repo_prior_art.cite: pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1: support-function interval for bounded DID distortions; pid_dose_response_shape_v1: finite-grid shape-closed outer bound

published_prior_art.cite: Manski2003 general set ID; KimKwonKwonLee2018 smoothness-based partial ID for treatment response; DCDHK2026NoUntreated no-untreated continuous DID
KimKwonKwonLee2018 gives smoothness-based treatment-response bounds, but not a DID common-trend derivative support-gap envelope
DCDHPSVB2025ContinuousEveryPeriod identifies switcher slopes from same-baseline stayers; DCDHK2026NoUntreated identifies local slopes using quasi-untreated controls

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The checklist omits KimKwonKwonLee2018, the closest smoothness-based partial-ID comparator for treatment-response bounds; the flagship-tier claim is unsupported without separating the DID-intercept contribution from generic smoothness interpolation.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; to reach flagship, the proposal needs a clearly new regime-opening theorem such as a generic multi-gap sharp algorithm with a published open-problem comparator, not only a one-gap deterministic C^{1,1} envelope.

soundness_flags.label: Formal setup / Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The locked block says common untreated trend conditional on X, but the main feasible class uses an undefined marginal m_P(d) and a scalar intercept c; after marginalizing over X, c(X) generally becomes E[c(X)|D=d], not a dose-invariant intercept.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The flagship formula assumes an interior gap with two observed endpoint values m_P(a), m_P(b), but the abstract and proof route also target no-near-zero boundary gaps where one endpoint need not be observed.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add KimKwonKwonLee2018 and any Whitney/Lipschitz-extension comparator to Related work, then state exactly what the DID common-trend intercept adds beyond generic smoothness-based partial identification.
Make the X convention single-valued: either remove X from v1, state all results conditional on each x and aggregate after bounding, or add assumptions making the scalar marginal intercept valid.
Extend Conjecture 1 from a two-endpoint interior gap to a boundary-gap and multi-gap computable sharp envelope, with an explicit finite-dimensional optimization problem and falsification conditions.

recommended_next_step: Revise the proposal to fix the X/intercept and boundary-gap statements, then strengthen the literature comparison and kernel so the result can plausibly clear the flagship floor.
